task_completion.description: Verify that the world_survey project is opened in the QField application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField app interface is visible, displaying a map, and the project name 'world_survey' is seen in the recent projects list or active session.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'pipelines' layer is located and selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField side menu or layer panel is open, and the 'pipelines' layer is visible and/or highlighted.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent activated editing or digitizing mode for the layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField interface shows active editing controls, such as a pencil icon toggled on, a crosshair in the center of the screen, or vertex editing tools.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Cairo-Baghdad' pipeline feature is selected for modification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The straight line representing the pipeline between Cairo and Baghdad is highlighted, or its start/end vertex nodes are visible, indicating it is ready for vertex editing.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new vertex is added to the pipeline at Amman, Jordan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map crosshair or a new vertex node is positioned over Amman (approximate coordinates 31.95°N, 35.93°E), and the line is pulled away from its original straight path to connect to this new point.

task_completion.description: Verify that the pipeline is finalized as a single feature with exactly three vertices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final map view shows the pipeline as a continuous bent line (V-shape) connecting Cairo, Amman, and Baghdad with exactly one middle vertex. The editing session appears completed or saved.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the QField graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show interaction with the QField app's map and menus. There should be no terminal emulators (like Termux) or scripts running spatial commands (e.g., ogr2ogr, sqlite3).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent visually navigated the map to locate the correct geographic area.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show panning and zooming on the map over the Middle East region (Egypt, Jordan, Iraq) to manually locate Amman.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the GIS software by directly editing the GeoPackage database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of database viewer apps, text editors, or code editors modifying the world_survey.gpkg file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying the 'Cairo-Baghdad' feature in the 'pipelines' layer of the world_survey.gpkg project. The target location for the new vertex is Amman, Jordan (coordinates: 31.95°N, 35.93°E, tolerance 0.5°). The final feature must be a single line with exactly 3 vertices (Cairo, Amman, Baghdad). Verify that the line is bent to pass through Amman and is not just a straight line, and that no extra vertices were added.
